analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
analysis.dtype: fp32 (TF32 tensorop path on pointwise GEMMs)

bottleneck.description: The single largest cost (34.0% of attributed time) is the standalone BatchNorm+ReLU6 epilogue. Inductor lowered each conv (cudnn/cutlass) separately from its BN, then emitted a memory-bound Triton kernel per block (triton_poi_fused__native_batch_norm_legit_no_training_hardtanh_{1..4}) that reads the full conv output, applies the affine (x-mean)*invstd*gamma+beta, clamps to [0,6], and writes the result back. At inference BN is a fixed per-channel affine transform, so this is redundant memory traffic that can be folded into the preceding convolution's weight/bias. The largest instances (hardtanh_4, the 256-channel output BN) run at 82.75% DRAM throughput with only 17.3% SM throughput and eligible_cycles 19.05% (Blackwell latency/memory-bound), confirming the kernel does almost no compute relative to the bytes it moves.

transformation.description: Enable Inductor freezing for inference. With weights treated as constants, Inductor's conv-BN folding pass folds each _native_batch_norm_legit_no_training (running_mean/var/gamma/beta constant in eval mode) into the preceding convolution's weight and bias, eliminating the per-channel normalization arithmetic. The remaining ReLU6 (hardtanh clamp) is fused into the conv epilogue / a cheaper pointwise, removing the dedicated read-modify-write BN traffic. This is a lowering decision Inductor already owns, so it is an inductor_config 'pass', not hand-written FX surgery.
transformation.location: torch._inductor.config.freezing (set before torch.compile on the eval/no_grad model)
fx_steps: inductor_config.freezing = True            # enables constant-folding + conv-BN fold for inference
compiled = torch.compile(model, backend='inductor')  # BN folded into preceding conv weights/bias
transformation.code_hint: Model must be in eval() and run under no_grad/inference_mode for freezing to treat BN stats and weights as constants; verify the recompiled graph no longer contains aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training nodes.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates BN read+write of normalized tensors (~535 KB written + ~269 KB read across the 12 BN kernels); only the ReLU6 clamp, fused into conv output, remains.

optimizations.notes: Folding allocates new folded conv weight/bias buffers, so per the 'register_buffer after dtype promotion' rule freezing (inductor_config) must run after any dtype-promotion pass. Funnel order (functional -> aten -> inductor_config) already guarantees OPT-2/OPT-3 (aten) precede this; the folded weight therefore inherits bf16 if OPT-2 is applied. No within-level ordering constraint needed.

bottleneck.description: The pointwise 1x1 convolutions are lowered to cutlass_80 s1688gemm TF32 tensorop kernels that achieve only 19-45% tensor_core_active and a catastrophic ~8.3% achieved_occupancy. The 128->256 GEMMs (op26/op53) carry 224 registers/thread and the 32->64 / 64->128 GEMMs 140-228 registers/thread; this register footprint is what caps occupancy (sm__warps_active ~8%), and eligible_cycles 16-20% marks them latency-bound on Blackwell. Because the input dtype is FP32, cutlass selects the wide-register TF32 tensorop path (cutlass_80_*s1688gemm, the Ampere-generation kernel) rather than a native low-precision Blackwell GEMM. The depthwise convs and BN kernels are simultaneously memory-bound (DRAM 57-83%), so halving element width also directly cuts their byte traffic.

transformation.description: Promote conv/GEMM inputs from FP32 to bf16 (model-wide autocast or an explicit aten-level cast pass on convolution inputs). This routes the 1x1 pointwise convs onto a native bf16 tensor-core GEMM with a smaller register footprint (relieving the 8% occupancy wall and 224-reg pressure), raises effective tensor-core utilization above the current 19-45% TF32 ceiling, and halves DRAM traffic for the memory-bound depthwise and BN kernels. At the aten level each decomposed conv input gets a bf16 cast inserted before aten.convolution.
transformation.location: aten graph: insert bf16 cast on inputs of torch.ops.aten.convolution.default (or wrap forward in torch.autocast('cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16))
transformation.code_hint: bf16 changes numerics; validate output tolerance against the FP32 reference. Keep BN/reduction accumulation in fp32 (autocast already does this). On Blackwell prefer bf16 over fp16 for range safety.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: ~2x reduction in activation byte traffic for depthwise + BN kernels; relieves register-bound occupancy on the pointwise GEMMs, enabling higher tensor-core throughput.

optimizations.notes: Capped at medium: accuracy-dependent and exa-search/context7 unavailable to confirm the exact Blackwell bf16 cutlass kernel selection. Must precede OPT-1 (freezing) by level order so the folded conv weight is allocated in bf16; already guaranteed by the funnel running aten before inductor_config.

bottleneck.description: triton_poi_fused_convolution_0 (4.91% of time, 2 launches) is a pure layout-conversion copy: grid [98,16,1], tensor_core 0%, sm_throughput 9.2%, eligible_cycles 8.6% (deeply latency/memory-bound), moving the FP32 input with no arithmetic. It exists because the model runs in default NCHW (channels_first) while every conv kernel in the graph is the NHWC variant (conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc), so Inductor inserts NCHW->NHWC permute copies at the boundary. Running the whole stack in channels_last eliminates this copy and lets the depthwise kernels (already memory-bound at 57-74% DRAM, occupancy 73-85%) read fully coalesced NHWC activations.

transformation.description: Convert the model and input to torch.channels_last (NHWC) memory format. Because the conv library kernels are already NHWC-native, this removes the standalone NCHW->NHWC copy kernel(s) entirely and improves memory coalescing for the depthwise convs and BN epilogues. At the aten level this is a memory-format annotation on the input placeholder and weights rather than an op rewrite.
transformation.location: aten graph: annotate input + conv weights with channels_last; equivalently model.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last) and x = x.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last)
fx_steps: # Inductor then propagates NHWC through the stack and drops the boundary permute copy
transformation.code_hint: Verify the recompiled graph no longer contains the triton_poi_fused_convolution_0 layout-copy node; confirm depthwise conv output stays NHWC end-to-end so no intermediate re-permute is reintroduced.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes the full-tensor NCHW->NHWC copy (read+write of the first-block input) and improves coalescing on the depthwise/BN kernels.

optimizations.notes: Independent of OPT-1/OPT-2 in effect but, being aten-level, naturally precedes OPT-1 (inductor_config) in funnel order. Capped at medium because the exact set of permute copies Inductor inserts could not be confirmed against fx-patterns.md (knowledge file unavailable in this environment).

global_notes: 0 operators below the 1% threshold omitted (combined 0.0% of attributed time); all 14 attributed operators are >= 1%.

Knowledge files (knowledge/hardware-limits.md, knowledge/fx-patterns.md) were not present in this environment; SM count and ridge-point limits were taken from built-in Blackwell knowledge (RTX PRO 6000 / GB202 ~188 SMs) rather than the repository table. Treat the hardware-limit-derived figures as approximate.

MCP tools (context7, exa-search, memory, sequential-thinking) were unavailable; per fallback policy, FX API patterns rely on built-in knowledge and novel/accuracy-dependent transforms (OPT-2 bf16, OPT-3 channels_last) are capped at medium confidence.

Blackwell removes warp_cycles_per_instruction (null throughout); latency-bound diagnosis uses eligible_cycles_pct < 20 instead. The pointwise GEMMs (eligible_cycles 16-20%, occupancy ~8%) are register-bound and latency-bound.
duration_ns values are inflated 2-5x by ncu counter collection; all estimated_impact figures are relative reductions within this profile, not absolute latencies.

edge_case_flag fused_kernel_double_count: many operators carry is_fused=true (cudnn_convolution wraps _convolution/convolution; BN op fuses hardtanh). Per-op durations reflect the fused kernel, so the fused_with members are not double-counted in the budget.

edge_case_flag warm_up_inflation: most conv operators have kernel_count=1 which is not a multiple of measure_iters (=2). This is a false positive from per-iteration NVTX op separation (each forward iteration produced distinct op_ids 6-26 and 33-53), not warm-up contamination; warm-up kernels k_00000-k_00345 are correctly isolated in warnings[] and excluded.

edge_case_flag dynamic_shapes: kernel_name conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc shows >50% duration CV, but this is driven by different channel counts (32/64/128) sharing one kernel name, not by truly dynamic shapes; shapes are static.
The model effectively executes 2 forward iterations (measure_iters=2): op_ids 6-26 are blocks 1-3 of iteration 1, op_ids 33-53 of iteration 2. Optimization impact applies per iteration.
Dependency DAG: OPT-2 (aten, bf16) -> OPT-1 (inductor_config, freezing) by funnel level order so the folded conv weight is bf16. OPT-3 (aten, channels_last) is independent. No cycles.
